_comment: XClaw config. Single file at ~/.xclaw/config.json. Top-level apiUrl/agent/rateLimit/context are shared defaults; each bots[] entry overrides them. Per-bot persona/behavior lives in SOUL.md + AGENTS.md under ~/.xclaw/<id>/. Secrets (octoToken, agent.gatewayToken) are OPTIONAL here: the macOS app stores them in the Keychain and injects them at runtime (secret.inject). Set them inline only for headless/no-GUI deployments.

agent._comment: Shared defaults for every bot; override per bot in bots[].
agent._comment_cron: Set cron=true to enable per-bot scheduled tasks (#115): owner-gated cron.create/list/delete over the control bus, fired through the gateway. Off by default.
agent._comment_toolProgress: Opt-in: when true, the bot mirrors each tool it runs to the channel as a brief '🔧 Running <tool>(<params>)…' notice (deduped, capped at 10/turn). Off by default.
agent._comment_inheritUserConfig: When true, the agent inherits the operator's ~/.claude (user-scope skills + installed plugins). OFF by default: each bot gets an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (~/.xclaw/<id>/.claude) so operator plugins/user-skills don't leak into every bot. The bot's own skills/workflows live under ~/.xclaw/<id>/.claude/{skills,workflows} and the CLI auto-loads them as user-scope; built-in CLI skills still load. Set true only for a trusted single-operator deployment.
agent._comment_dispatchTimeoutSec: Per-turn IDLE timeout (seconds) — resets on every AgentEvent, so a long-running healthy turn (multi-agent workflow, big stream, many tool calls) survives as long as events flow; only N seconds of silence kills it. Omit to use the daemon default (1200 = 20 min). Bump higher when the bot routinely runs long silent tools (e.g. a slow Bash); lower for snappy DMs where a stuck turn should surface fast.

_comment_groupConfigDir: Optional directory of operator-authored per-conversation instruction files. For a group channel <channelId>, the file <groupConfigDir>/<channelId>.md is injected into the system prompt as a trusted [Group instructions] block (capped at 32 KiB). A thread channel '<groupNo>____<shortId>' prefers <groupNo>____<shortId>.md and falls back to <groupNo>.md. SECURITY: these files are injected UNSANITIZED, so the dir MUST be operator-controlled and OUTSIDE the agent-writable workspace — config load rejects a groupConfigDir at or under a bot's cwdBase, and group/world-writable files are refused. Settable top-level (shared) and per bot (override).
_comment_gating: Optional group-gating policy (session-router): mentionFreeGroups = channel ids the bot answers WITHOUT an @mention; knownBotUids/allowedBotUids = the bot-loop guard (uids ending in _bot or in knownBotUids are dropped in DMs and mention-free groups unless in allowedBotUids); botBlocklist = uids whose DMs are dropped. All are top-level defaults; a bots[] entry may override each (the per-bot value REPLACES the default).
_comment_skills: Skills/workflows are NOT configured here. Each bot owns its own under ~/.xclaw/<id>/.claude/skills/<name>/ (SKILL.md bundles) and ~/.xclaw/<id>/.claude/workflows/<name>.js — the claude CLI auto-loads them as user-scope via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR. There is no shared marketplace, no install concept. Author/edit them in the desktop app (gear → per-bot Skills / Workflows). Any leftover top-level or per-bot "skills"/"workflows" keys in this file are ignored.

bots._comment_overrides: apiUrl/agent/rateLimit/context here override the top-level defaults for this bot only.

agent._comment_env: Extra env vars injected into THIS bot's agent CLI. Per-bot identity vars like OCTO_BOT_ID belong here. agent.env merges over the top-level agent.env by key.

bots._comment_persona: Persona clone (openclaw OBO): replies in the grantor's voice. Triggered when the grantor is @-mentioned or the group is @所有人. uid is the grantor's server uid (the security anchor — only OBO fields signed by this uid are trusted); name is woven into the persona system prompt; personaPrompt is an optional free-form instruction.

onBehalfOf.name: Admin
onBehalfOf.personaPrompt: Always reply concisely and in English.
